trade.summary: Korea-Netherlands trade is critically shaped by ASML's dominant position in semiconductor lithography — Samsung and SK Hynix are among ASML's largest customers, purchasing EUV and DUV machines for their advanced chip fabs in Korea. This makes the Netherlands one of South Korea's most strategically important technology partners. Both countries benefit from the EU-Korea FTA (2011).
top_products_a_to_b: semiconductors and memory chips (Samsung, SK Hynix)
disputes: Korean chipmakers affected by Dutch export restrictions on ASML tools to China, though Korea itself is an approved buyer

diplomatic.summary: Korea-Netherlands relations are strong and increasingly strategically significant due to the semiconductor technology nexus. Both are liberal democracies and members of multilateral economic institutions. They cooperate on technology export controls, climate policy, and trade. The EU-Korea FTA provides a solid legal framework. Netherlands is an important EU partner for Korea.

military.summary: Both countries are US security partners. The Netherlands participated in the Korean War under UN command (a point of historical connection). Growing alignment on technology security. No formal bilateral defense treaty. Both countries support Ukraine against Russia.
military.alliance_status: Aligned — Netherlands NATO, Korea US ally

financial.summary: Significant bilateral investment driven by semiconductor equipment and technology. Samsung and SK Hynix have purchasing and collaborative R&D relationships with ASML. Dutch institutional investors hold Korean equities. Korean conglomerates have European headquarters considerations in the Netherlands due to its favorable tax and legal environment.

energy.summary: Limited direct bilateral energy trade. Shell (Netherlands) has some LNG supply relationships with Korean buyers. Both countries cooperate on hydrogen and offshore wind technology.

scientific.summary: Strong science and technology cooperation centered on semiconductors. Samsung, SK Hynix, and Korean research institutes have joint R&D with ASML and Dutch research institutions. Cooperation in photonics, nanotechnology, and materials science is substantial. Both countries are significant R&D spenders.
